title: junellia juniperina var. campestris
intro: Junellia juniperina var. campestris, also known as 'Molle' or 'Small-leaved Juniper', is a small shrub native to the Andean region of South America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids, contributing to its medicinal value. Junellia juniperina var. campestris can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which have been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues, soothe digestive problems, and support the immune system.
